title: Urban VPN Review 2026: Free but Dangerous — Why We Cannot Recommend It
metaDescription: Our honest Urban VPN review for 2026 covers real speed tests, the AI chat data scandal, P2P privacy risks, streaming performance, and why free VPNs can cost you more than you think.

testResults.downloadSpeed: 24 Mbps average on US servers (109 Mbps baseline)
testResults.speedRetention: 12% on distant servers, up to 93% on nearby servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Claims no-logs but collects IP addresses, browsing history, geolocation, and device identifiers. Never independently audited.

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict Urban VPN is a free VPN service that we **cannot recommend** to anyone who values their privacy, security, or online safety.
Pro Tip: Before you settle for any free VPN, read our best free VPN guide — there are genuinely safe free options that do not harvest your data.
After thorough testing and research, it earns a **2.8/10** from us — one of our lowest ratings. While the free price tag is appealing, Urban VPN operates a peer-to-peer network that routes other users' traffic through your device, collects and sells your personal data to third parties, and was caught harvesting private AI chatbot conversations from 8 million users in 2025. The security risks far outweigh any benefits. **Best for:** Absolutely no one. There are better free alternatives like Proton VPN Free or Windscribe Free that do not compromise your privacy. **Key strengths:** Completely free with no account required, available on multiple platforms, can unblock Disney+ and YouTube TV.
💡 Pro Tip: Urban VPN is 100% free but uses a peer-to-peer model — other users' bandwidth is used to route your traffic, and your bandwidth may be used to route others'. This creates privacy and security risks that paid VPNs do not have. Never use Urban VPN for sensitive activities like banking, logging into accounts, or sharing personal files. For free options with traditional VPN infrastructure, use ProtonVPN Free (unlimited data) or Windscribe Free (10 GB/month).
**Key weaknesses:** Peer-to-peer architecture exposes you to legal liability, collects and sells your browsing data, caught stealing AI chat conversations, no kill switch on free plan, extremely slow speeds on distant servers, US jurisdiction under Five Eyes surveillance alliance. --- ## Speed Test Results Urban VPN's speed performance is among the worst we have tested. Because the free plan uses a peer-to-peer (P2P) network model — where your traffic is routed through other users' devices rather than dedicated servers — speeds are wildly inconsistent and depend entirely on the quality of the exit node you are assigned. ### Our Test Environment - **Base connection:** 109 Mbps download (fiber) - **Testing tool:** Ookla Speedtest CLI, repeated 3x per server - **Protocol:** OpenVPN (only option available) - **Date tested:** March 2026 ### Results by Server Location | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Speed Retention | |----------------|----------------|---------------|-----------|----------------| | Local (nearest) | 101 | 18 | 45 | 93% | | US - New York | 24 | 8 | 89 | 22% | | US - San Francisco | 26.4 | 9 | 112 | 24% | | UK - London | 15 | 5 | 156 | 14% | | Japan - Tokyo | 8 | 3 | 287 | 7% | | Australia - Sydney | 25 | 6 | 234 | 23% | **Average across all tested servers:** 33 Mbps download with massive inconsistency. On nearby servers, Urban VPN retained about 93% of our base speed — decent for basic browsing. But on any server outside our region, speeds dropped by 76% to 93%, making streaming, video calls, and large downloads essentially unusable. The fundamental problem is the P2P architecture. Every time you connect, you are routed through a different user's device. If that person has a slow connection, your VPN connection is equally slow. We saw download speeds swing from 101 Mbps to 8 Mbps within minutes when reconnecting to the same server location. ### Urban VPN vs NordVPN vs Proton VPN Free: Speed Comparison | Metric | Urban VPN | NordVPN | Proton VPN Free | |--------|-----------|---------|----------------| | Avg. Download Speed | 33 Mbps | 780 Mbps | 86 Mbps | | Avg. Upload Speed | 8 Mbps | 698 Mbps | 42 Mbps | | Speed Retention | 30% | 87% | 79% | | Best Protocol | OpenVPN | NordLynx | WireGuard | | Price | Free | $3.39/mo | Free | Urban VPN is one of the slowest VPNs we tested in 2026. Even compared to other free VPNs, Proton VPN Free delivers 2.5x faster average speeds on a more reliable network with no P2P routing. --- ## Security & Privacy This is where Urban VPN fails catastrophically. What should be a VPN's core purpose — protecting your privacy — is exactly what Urban VPN undermines. ### The P2P Network Problem Urban VPN's free tier does not use dedicated servers. Instead, it operates a peer-to-peer network where every user simultaneously acts as an exit node for other users' traffic. This means: 1. **Other people's internet traffic is routed through your device and your IP address.** If another Urban VPN user connected through your device visits illegal content, downloads copyrighted material, or engages in any criminal activity, it appears to come from your IP address. You could be held legally responsible. 2. **Your device resources are consumed.** Urban VPN uses your bandwidth, processing power, and battery to serve other users' connections. Multiple reviewers reported 500 MB to 2 GB of additional daily data usage and a 15-20% increase in battery drain on mobile devices. 3. **You have no control over who uses your connection.** There is no way to opt out of being an exit node on the free plan while still using the VPN. ### Data Collection and Sale Urban VPN's privacy policy reveals an extensive data collection operation that directly contradicts the purpose of a VPN: - **IP addresses** are logged - **Browsing history** and online activity are tracked - **Device identifiers** including hardware model, operating system, and unique device IDs - **Geolocation data** including approximate physical location - **Connection timestamps** and session duration This data is shared with BiScience (B.I Science (2009) Ltd.), an affiliated data broker company that specializes in digital marketing intelligence and data extraction. BiScience monetizes this data by selling browsing behavior insights to advertisers and marketing firms. Essentially, you are the product. ### The AI Chat Harvesting Scandal (2025) In December 2025, security researchers discovered that Urban VPN's browser extension (version 5.5.0, released July 2025) had been silently harvesting complete AI chatbot conversations from approximately 8 million users. The extension captured every prompt and response from ChatGPT, Claude, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, Perplexity, DeepSeek, Grok, and Meta AI. Key facts about this scandal: - The data collection was introduced via a silent update
Pro Tip: Your IP address, browsing activity, and even AI conversations can be exposed by a bad VPN. Our VPN privacy guide explains exactly what to look for before trusting any provider.
— users who installed before July 2025 never saw an updated consent prompt - The harvesting worked even when the VPN features were disabled - There was no user-facing toggle to disable the AI conversation collection - The only way to stop the data theft was to completely uninstall the extension - The harvested conversations were sent to BiScience's servers for monetization - Google removed all four Urban VPN extensions from the Chrome Web Store in December 2025 - Microsoft Edge versions remained available longer, though the Featured badge was eventually removed This incident was reported by Koi Security, Malwarebytes, The Hacker News, CSO Online, Dark Reading, Infosecurity Magazine, and The Register, among other cybersecurity outlets. ### Encryption & Protocols - **AES-256 encryption** via OpenVPN — this is the only protocol available - **No WireGuard** support — the fastest modern protocol is absent - **No IKEv2/IPsec** — no alternative protocol options - **No proprietary protocol** — unlike NordVPN (NordLynx) or ExpressVPN (Lightway) ### Missing Security Features - **No kill switch** on the free plan — if the VPN connection drops (which happens frequently), your real IP address is immediately exposed - **No split tunneling** (except limited Windows 10 support) - **No multi-hop routing** — traffic passes through only one node - **No obfuscated servers** — easily detected and blocked - **No independent security audits** — Urban VPN has never been audited by a third party ### Jurisdiction Urban VPN is operated by Urban Cyber Security Inc., headquartered in **Manhattan, New York, United States**. The US is a founding member of the Five Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance, which means the US government can compel companies to hand over user data — and Urban VPN already collects plenty of it. --- ## Streaming Performance Streaming is not Urban VPN's strength. The combination of slow speeds and inconsistent P2P connections makes reliable streaming difficult, and most major platforms detect and block Urban VPN's IP addresses. ### Platform-by-Platform Results | Platform | Unblocked? | Notes | |----------|-----------|-------| | Netflix US | No | Blocked. Received "You seem to be using a VPN or proxy" error on every US server tested. | | BBC iPlayer | No | Unable to access UK content. Blocked on all tested UK servers. | | Disney+ | Yes | Worked. Able to stream The Mandalorian in UHD without major issues. | | Amazon Prime Video | No | Detected and blocked. | | Hulu | No | Blocked on all tested US servers. | | HBO Max / Max | Yes | Worked. HD streaming was buffer-free on nearby servers. | | YouTube TV | Yes | Worked with acceptable quality. | | ESPN+ | No | Blocked. | | DAZN | No | Not tested due to speed limitations. | | Peacock | No | Blocked. | Urban VPN only reliably unblocked 3 out of 10 tested streaming platforms — a failure rate of 70%. Even on platforms that worked, the slow and inconsistent speeds meant frequent quality drops and occasional buffering on distant servers. For streaming, NordVPN or Surfshark are vastly superior choices that unblock virtually every major platform. --- ## Pricing & Plans Urban VPN's main draw is its free tier, but the premium option offers dedicated servers for users willing to pay. ### Pricing Overview (March 2026) | Plan | Price | Billed As | |------|-------|-----------| | **Free** | $0/mo | Free forever | | **Premium 1 Month** | $9.56/mo | $9.56 billed monthly | | **Premium 6 Months** | $4.17/mo | $25.02 billed every 6 months (save 56%) | | **Premium 1 Year** | $3.08/mo | $36.96 billed annually (save 68%) | ### What Each Tier Includes - **Free Plan:** Full server access via P2P network across 82 countries, unlimited data, no account required. However, your data is collected and sold, your device serves as an exit node for other users, and there is no kill switch. - **Premium Plan:** Access to 652+ dedicated servers in 89 countries, up to 8 simultaneous device connections, the promise that no data is collected (though this has never been independently verified), and a 30-day money-back guarantee. Currently available on Android and iOS with Windows and Mac desktop apps listed as "coming soon." ### Is It Worth the Price? No. At $3.08/month for the annual plan, Urban VPN Premium is priced comparably to NordVPN ($3.39/month) and Surfshark ($1.99/month) — both of which offer vastly superior speed, security, audited no-logs policies, and reliable streaming. Urban VPN Premium only works on mobile devices for now, lacks independent audits to verify its "no data collection" promise, and the company's track record of harvesting user data makes any privacy promise difficult to trust. The free plan is even worse value. You pay nothing in dollars but pay everything in privacy. Your browsing data, device information, and even your AI conversations have been monetized. There are genuinely free VPN alternatives — Proton VPN Free offers servers in 5 countries with no data collection, no ads, and a verified no-logs policy. ### Payment Methods Urban VPN Premium accepts credit/debit cards and PayPal. No cryptocurrency option is available for anonymous payment. --- ## Apps & Usability Urban VPN is available across multiple platforms, though the experience varies significantly. ### Supported Platforms - **Desktop:** Windows, macOS (free P2P plan only; Premium desktop apps listed as "coming soon") - **Mobile:** Android, iOS (both free and Premium) - **Browser Extensions:** Chrome and Edge extensions were removed from the Chrome Web Store in December 2025 following the AI chat harvesting scandal. Edge versions were delisted later. ### Simultaneous Connections - **Free Plan:** Unlimited devices - **Premium Plan:** Up to 8 devices ### User Interface
💡 Pro Tip: If you chose Urban VPN because you cannot afford a paid VPN, consider that PrivadoVPN and Hide.me both offer legitimate free plans with 10 GB/month data — no peer bandwidth sharing required. For the most affordable paid VPN, see our best cheap VPN guide where options start under $2/month.
The desktop app features a simple map-based interface where you select a country to connect. The design is clean but basic — there are no advanced settings, no protocol selection (OpenVPN is the only option), and no split tunneling controls. The Windows app showed only 56 confirmed server locations despite advertising 4,500+ servers. The mobile apps are functional but have stability issues. Multiple reviewers reported frequent disconnections on iOS and app crashes on macOS. ### Known Issues - **Battery drain:** 15-20% additional daily battery consumption on mobile due to P2P network activity - **Data consumption:** 500 MB to 2 GB of extra daily data usage from serving other users' traffic - **iOS disconnections:** Frequent VPN drops that expose your real IP (no kill switch on free plan) - **macOS crashes:** Desktop client instability reported across multiple review sites - **Customer support:** No live chat. Only self-help articles and an email contact form with slow response times. --- ## Comparison with Alternatives To put Urban VPN in context, here is how it compares against two popular alternatives — one premium and one free. | Feature | Urban VPN (Free) | NordVPN | Proton VPN Free | |---------|-----------------|---------|----------------| | Price | $0 | $3.39/mo | $0 | | Servers | P2P network | 7,000+ dedicated | 200+ dedicated | | Countries | 82 | 118 | 5 | | Speed (avg download) | 33 Mbps | 780 Mbps | 86 Mbps | | Kill Switch | No | Yes | Yes | | No-Logs Audits | None | 5 (Deloitte, PwC) | 2 (Securitum) | | Protocols | OpenVPN only | NordLynx, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | | Netflix US | No | Yes | No (free tier) | | Data Collection | Extensive | None (audited) | None (audited) | | Jurisdiction | US (Five Eyes) | Panama | Switzerland | | P2P Exit Node Risk | Yes | No | No | | Simultaneous Connections | Unlimited | 10 | 1 | | Independent Audits | None | 5+ | 2 | Even as a free VPN, Urban VPN is outclassed by Proton VPN Free in every metric that matters: speed, privacy, security features, and trustworthiness. If you are willing to pay, NordVPN and Surfshark offer premium experiences at comparable prices to Urban VPN Premium. --- ## What We Do Not Like To be direct about Urban VPN's most serious problems: 1. **Your device becomes an exit node.** This is the single biggest red flag. Other users' traffic — potentially illegal — flows through your IP address. No other mainstream VPN does this without explicit consent. 2. **Caught stealing AI conversations.** The 2025 scandal where Urban VPN harvested 8 million users' private AI chats from ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and other platforms is a fundamental breach of trust. The data collection was silent, had no opt-out toggle, and continued even when VPN features were disabled. 3. **Data is collected and sold.** Despite being marketed as a privacy tool, Urban VPN collects IP addresses, browsing history, geolocation, and device identifiers, then shares this data with BiScience for monetization. 4. **No independent audits.** Urban VPN has never subjected its infrastructure, code, or privacy practices to third-party verification. Every privacy claim is unverifiable. 5. **US jurisdiction.** Operating under Five Eyes surveillance means the US government can compel data disclosure — and Urban VPN has plenty of data to disclose. 6. **Painfully slow on distant servers.** Speed drops of 76% to 93% on international servers make Urban VPN unsuitable for streaming, gaming, or any bandwidth-intensive activity. 7. **No kill switch on free plan.** When the connection drops — which happens often — your real IP is exposed instantly. --- ## Who Should Use Urban VPN? ### Honestly, No One We cannot identify a use case where Urban VPN is the best available option. Even for users who cannot afford a paid VPN: - **Proton VPN Free** offers servers in 5 countries with no data logging, no ads, and a verified no-logs policy — and it is equally free. - **Windscribe Free** provides 10 GB/month with servers in 10+ countries and a transparent privacy policy. - **hide.me Free** offers 10 GB/month with no connection logs. All three free alternatives are faster, safer, and more private than Urban VPN. ### Definitely Avoid If You Are - **Privacy-conscious** — Urban VPN collects more data than many of the trackers you are trying to avoid - **A journalist, activist, or whistleblower** — the P2P architecture and data logging make this VPN actively dangerous - **A streamer** — only 3 of 10 tested platforms work, and speeds are too inconsistent for reliable viewing - **Using AI chatbots** — the extension was literally caught stealing your conversations - **On a metered data plan** — the P2P network consumes 500 MB to 2 GB of extra data daily --- ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Is Urban VPN safe to use in 2026? No. Urban VPN has significant safety concerns. It operates a peer-to-peer network that routes other users' traffic through your device and IP address, potentially exposing you to legal liability. The service collects and sells browsing data, IP addresses, and geolocation information through its affiliate BiScience. In December 2025, its browser extension was caught silently harvesting AI chatbot conversations from 8 million users. It has never undergone an independent security audit. ### Is Urban VPN really free? The desktop apps are free to download and use with no account required. However, you pay with your privacy — Urban VPN collects your browsing data, IP address, device information, and geolocation, then monetizes this data through its partner BiScience. Your device also serves as an exit node for other users' traffic, consuming your bandwidth and battery. A Premium plan with dedicated servers starts at $3.08/month billed annually. ### Does Urban VPN work with Netflix? No. In our March 2026 tests, Urban VPN was unable to unblock Netflix US. Every US server we tested returned Netflix's VPN detection error. Urban VPN did work with Disney+, Max (HBO Max), and YouTube TV, but failed on Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, Hulu, BBC iPlayer, and most other major platforms. ### What happened with Urban VPN and AI chats? In December 2025, researchers at Koi Security discovered that Urban VPN's browser extension (version 5.5.0, released July 2025) was silently intercepting and exporting complete AI chat conversations from ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Perplexity, DeepSeek, Grok, and Meta AI. The harvesting affected approximately 8 million users, was enabled by default with no opt-out toggle, and worked even when VPN features were disabled. Google removed the extensions from the Chrome Web Store in December 2025. ### How does Urban VPN's P2P network work? Unlike traditional VPNs that route your traffic through dedicated servers, Urban VPN's free tier uses a peer-to-peer model where every user simultaneously acts as a VPN server (exit node) for other users. When you connect, your traffic exits through another user's device and IP address, and their traffic exits through yours. This means if another user performs illegal activity while routed through your device, it appears to originate from your IP address. You cannot opt out of being an exit node while using the free plan. ### Is Urban VPN better than Proton VPN Free? No. Proton VPN Free is superior in every meaningful way. Proton VPN Free uses dedicated servers (not P2P), has a verified no-logs policy audited by Securitum, offers WireGuard protocol support, includes a kill switch, and is based in Switzerland — outside the Five Eyes alliance. Proton VPN Free is limited to servers in 5 countries and 1 device connection, but it does not collect your data or use your device as an exit node. ### Can Urban VPN be used for torrenting? We strongly advise against it. Urban VPN's P2P architecture means your real IP address could be exposed during a VPN connection drop (there is no kill switch on the free plan), and the service logs your IP address and browsing activity. Multiple review sites confirm that Urban VPN does not support or optimize for P2P/torrent traffic. ### Does Urban VPN have a kill switch? The free plan does not include a kill switch, which is a critical security feature that blocks all internet traffic if the VPN connection drops. Without it, your real IP address is exposed every time the connection fails — and Urban VPN's P2P connections are notably unstable. The Premium plan reportedly includes a kill switch, but this has not been independently verified.
